name: The Dog House
known_for: 1881 tavern bar, 1923 player piano, water views of Saratoga Passage
significance: Only National Register building in Langley. Name from Prohibition gambling — husbands came home Monday "in the dog house." First movie theater in Langley 1922-1937.

name: Ivar's Acres of Clams
known_for: Clam chowder, fish and chips — the flagship
significance: Fourth of Jul-Ivar's fireworks 1964-2008, 300K attendees. Haglund owned Smith Tower. Underwater billboard hoax 2009.

name: Totem Family Dining
known_for: Competition BBQ, retro counter, neon totem sign
significance: Oldest continuously operating restaurant in Everett (78+ years). Best Breakfast every year since 2014 (Herald).

name: Dick's Drive-In
known_for: No-substitution burgers, Dick's Deluxe, 8K tuition benefit
founded_by: Dick Spady, H. Warren Ghormley, Dr. B.O.A. Thomas
significance: Most life-changing burger joint in America (Esquire 2013). Sir Mix-a-Lot, Macklemore. 10 locations, 8M revenue.

name: Beth's Cafe
known_for: 12-egg omelet on pizza tray, 450K+ eggs/year, patron wall art
significance: Man v. Food, 1000 Places to See Before You Die. Last true greasy-spoon on Aurora. Punk/grunge cultural fixture.

name: Bakeman's Restaurant
known_for: Turkey sandwiches on homemade bread, cafeteria-style, strict ordering
founded_by: Matthew Wang; run by Jason Wang
significance: Seattle's Sandwich Nazi. 300-400 daily. Eulogized as death of old Seattle by Times, Cascade PBS, KNKX.

name: Cabbage Patch Restaurant
significance: Heart of Antique Capitol of the World. Building was residence, boarding house, antique store with Native artifacts.

name: The Crumpet Shop
address: Corner Market Building, First & Pike Place
known_for: English griddle cakes, loose-leaf tea, smoked salmon crumpet
founded_by: Gary Lasater (orig: The United Metropolitan Improved Crumpet Baking & Punctual Delivery Company)
significance: Nearly 50 years continuous in Pike Place. Atlantic called it best scone in the country. Second location in Edmonds.

name: Buzz Inn Steakhouse
known_for: American fare — burgers, chicken fried steak, steaks, pancakes
significance: 12 locations, 9 in Snohomish County. Generational family institution. Tackitt still majority owner.

name: Ezell's Famous Chicken
known_for: Hand-breaded fried chicken, Texas family recipes, homemade rolls
founded_by: Lewis Rudd, Faye Rudd, Ezell Stephens
significance: Oprah called it best fried chicken she ever had (1989). 17 locations. R.U.B.B. Initiative for Black-owned businesses.

name: Maltby Cafe
known_for: Giant cinnamon rolls (200/morning), 1937 schoolhouse, country breakfast
significance: Building is 1937 gym/cafeteria for 1907 school. Recipe came with restaurant, never changed. Regional destination.

name: Charles at Smugglers Cove
known_for: French fine dining with Possession Sound views
significance: Prohibition-era bootlegging building (1929). Hidden sub-basement still, tunnel to Sound. Connected to Roy Olmstead network.

name: Ivar's Mukilteo Landing
known_for: Clam chowder, fish and chips, salmon — full-service waterfront dining
founded_by: Ivar Haglund (brand 1938); site: saloon 1909, Taylor's Landing 1944
significance: Ferry landing site served food continuously since 1909. Taylor family were civic leaders. Ivar bought in 1991.

name: Mr. Bill's
known_for: 1950s retro diner, best pastrami in NW, huge chicken-fried steak
significance: Pink neon, Howdy Doody, red flocked wallpaper. Lost era of kitschy Aurora corridor diners.
